text.title: Server Actions: authoring, validation, and revalidation
text.summary: Server Actions replace POST API routes for form submissions; they run on the server, validate input with Zod, write to the DB, and call revalidatePath or redirect.

rule.statement: Place Server Actions in a dedicated actions.ts file colocated with the route (e.g., app/posts/new/actions.ts), not inlined in the page component. Add 'use server' at the top of the file, not per-function.
rule.rationale: Colocated actions.ts files are easier to test and review. File-level 'use server' marks all exports as server-only without requiring per-function directives.

rule.statement: Always validate Server Action input with Zod before touching the database. Return { error } on validation failure so the client can display field errors.
rule.rationale: Server Actions receive FormData which is untyped. Without Zod validation, invalid data reaches the DB silently.

section.title: redirect and notFound inside Server Actions

rule.statement: Do not wrap redirect() or notFound() calls in try/catch. Both functions throw internally to signal navigation; a try/catch block swallows the throw and navigation never happens.
rule.rationale: redirect() and notFound() use the NEXT_REDIRECT and NEXT_NOT_FOUND throw signals. Catching them silently breaks the navigation flow with no visible error.

section.title: When NOT to use Server Actions

rule.statement: Do not add 'use server' to functions called directly from Server Components — those are normal async functions with no RPC overhead. Server Actions only provide the RPC transport when called from client event handlers or form actions.
rule.rationale: Calling a 'use server' function from a Server Component is just a regular await; the 'use server' directive has no effect in that context and misleads readers.

text.reference: Server Action template with Zod, revalidatePath, redirect
Prompt for converting client-side form to Server Action
